steps.title: Identify unit prices from the equation coefficients
steps.content:
The equation $24.5x + 24.75y = 641$ represents a cost relationship. It has the form:
$$(\text{cost per unit of topsoil}) \cdot x + (\text{cost per unit of stone}) \cdot y = \text{total cost}$$
Comparing with our equation, the coefficient of $x$ is the cost per cubic yard of topsoil: \$24.50.
The coefficient of $y$ is the cost per ton of crushed stone: \$24.75.

steps.title: Find the price difference
steps.content:
Subtract the unit price of topsoil from the unit price of crushed stone:
$$24.75 - 24.5 = 0.25$$
A ton of crushed stone cost Isabel \$0.25 more than a cubic yard of topsoil.
